channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Three reasons that should drive your endpoint management strategy in 2026
item.description: 2026 will be the year many government IT teams either implement their first unified endpoint management (UEM) strategy or replace outdated ones entirely. The post Three reasons that should drive your endpoint management strategy in 2026 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: EVs just outsold gas cars in Europe for the first time
item.description: EVs hit a new milestone: In December, buyers in Europe registered more electric cars than gas cars for the first time. EV registrations hit 217,898 in the EU last month—up 50% year-over-year from 2024. Sales of gas cars, on the other hand, dropped nearly 20% to 216,492.
item.dc:creator: Adele Peters, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft is finally fixing Windows 11 under the hood
item.description: Microsoft is working to smooth out the inner workings of Windows 11, but I'm struggling to have any confidence in this effort.
item.dc:creator: Darren Allan, TechRadar

item.title: Newsom zeroes out California’s landmark deal to save local news
item.description: Local news has suffered a long decline since the advent of the internet shifted the ads newspapers and other outlets relied upon into the digital realm.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Meta-Corning fiber deal signals a new bottleneck in AI infrastructure
item.description: Meta has announced a $6 billion, multi-year fiber supply agreement with Corning, highlighting how AI infrastructure constraints are shifting beyond compute and into the physical network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Power shortages, carbon capture, and AI automation: What’s ahead for data centers in 2026
item.description: The data center industry is racing toward a power crisis that technology alone will not be able to solve, according to Uptime Institute’s 2026 data center predictions report.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How AI-induced cultural stagnation is already happening
item.description: Generative AI was trained on centuries of art and writing produced by humans. But scientists and critics have wondered what would happen once AI became widely adopted and started training on its outputs. A new study points to some answers.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Disruptive innovation is key to building world-changing companies, but it needs a moral compass in the age of AI
item.description: At the Consumer Electronics Show in early January, Razer made waves by unveiling a small jar containing a holographic anime bot designed to accompany gamers not just during gameplay, but in daily life.
item.dc:creator: Julia Freeland Fisher, Fast Company

item.title: How the Grammys are adapting in a world of AI music, ‘KPop Demon Hunters,’ and a changing music marketplace
item.description: The Grammy Awards return February 1 at a pivotal moment for the music industry, one shaped by trending Latin artists, resurgent rock legends, and even charting AI acts. To unpack what will make this year’s broadcast distinctive, the Recording Academy CEO Harvey Mason Jr.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company
